# 

# **PURPOSE** {#purpose}

This SOP ensures that every MEx Specialist delivers consistent, warm, and professional greetings and closings that reflect our **CARE** philosophy. The goal is to build trust in the first 30 seconds of any interaction, leave a lasting positive impression, and use whisper prompts to guide whether a General or Specialty Queue greeting and closing is required.

# **CONTEXT** {#context}

### **NEW\! Whisper Prompts – How They Work** {#new!-whisper-prompts-–-how-they-work}

* **What they are:** Short audio messages that play in your headset before the call connects.

* **Who hears them:** Only you, never the member.

* **Why they matter:** They identify which queue the call is coming from, helping you determine whether to use a **General Queue** or **Specialty Queue** greeting and closing.

Whisper prompts are especially important for **Specialty Schools** because they:

* Alert you to provide **white glove service**.

* Ensure that you include the school name in your greeting and closing.

* Remind you to follow any **chapter-specific rules, timelines, or procedures**.

# **RESOURCES** {#resources}

## Related Topics {#related-topics}

* [MEx Greeting and Closing Job Aid](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1n_MyqQNd_80DYGAhLQhcMg4a5ppARmfmHUJHUVPz1aI/edit?tab=t.0)  
* [Special Schools 8x8 Scripts](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-JISXMM2f_u3Hs5eM7Q9KE_CH1CLiZZA3eZ7_mgOlF8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.ujv9ebip1gnx)


## Definitions {#definitions}

* **CARE Philosophy** – The NSLS service framework that stands for **Connected, Attentive, Responsible, Enthusiastic**. Every interaction should demonstrate these values.  
* **Whisper Prompt** – A short audio message played in an agent’s headset before a call connects. It identifies the incoming call’s queue (General or Specialty) so the agent can deliver the correct greeting and closing.  
* **General Queue** – A phone line that is not associated with a specific school or chapter. Use the standard NSLS greeting and closing for these interactions.  
* **Specialty Queue** – A phone line dedicated to a specific school/chapter. Requires a greeting and closing that includes the school’s name and adheres to any special rules, timelines, or procedures.  
* **White Glove Service** – Providing an elevated level of care, attention, and personalization to Specialty Chapter members.  
* **Verification** – The process of confirming a caller’s identity before discussing account details or processing requests, to ensure privacy and security.  
* **Closing** – The wrap-up portion of a call where the agent offers further assistance, celebrates something with the member, thanks them for reaching out, and (for Specialty Queues) references both the chapter name and the NSLS.

## References  {#references}

* [SOP \-SNHU Spring 2025](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f-JS3eov_UsfLvkuBhEIH6g_8py6-D4i/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=111681120562640884507&rtpof=true&sd=true)

# **PROCESS** {#process}

## Step 1 {#step-1}

Listen for the whisper prompt before answering.

Determine if it’s a **General Queue** or **Specialty Queue**.

## Step 2 {#step-2}

Use the **General Queue** or **Specialty Queue** script as appropriate.

Tip: If you smile when you speak, it will come through in your tone.

**General Queue Greeting:**

 “Thank you for calling The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?”

* Alternate: “How may I assist you today?”

**Specialty Queue Greeting:**

 “Thank you for calling the \[School Name\] Member Experience Line for The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?”

* Alternate: “How may I assist you today?”

* *Always say the full school name.*

## Step 3 {#step-3}

* After they share their name, be sure to use their name and express your willingness to help:

   “Thank you, \[Member Name\], for calling today. I’m glad I received your call and I’ll do all I can to help you. First, I just need to ask a few questions to protect your privacy and security.”

* Complete verification by following the Call Verification SOP. (There is currently no link to this SOP)

* Thank them for verifying:

   “Thank you so much for helping me verify your account. What had you reach out to us today, and how can I help you?”

## Step 4 {#step-4}

Listen attentively, answer questions, and provide solutions.

Tip: If multiple questions are asked immediately, taking written note of all questions asked will help you remember and address each of the member’s concerns.

Use the CARE philosophy throughout the conversation.

## Step 5 {#step-5}

**General Queue Closing:**

 “It’s been great speaking with you today\! We appreciate you as a member and I’m always happy to help. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we wrap up? Thank you for reaching out, and have a great day from all of us at The NSLS.”

**Specialty Queue Closing:**

 “It’s been wonderful connecting with you today\! We appreciate you as a valued member of the \[School Name\] Chapter. Is there anything else I can help you with before we finish? Thank you for reaching out, and have a great day from the \[School Name\] Chapter and The NSLS.”

* *Always say the full school name in the closing.*

* You may abbreviate “The National Society of Leadership and Success” to “The NSLS” in closings.

If not already done earlier in the interaction, include a celebration if possible (e.g., nearing induction, joining this term, completing a step).

## **Queue Greeting & Closing Quick Reference** {#queue-greeting-&-closing-quick-reference}

*Always use the **full school name** in greetings and closings for Specialty Queues. You may abbreviate “The National Society of Leadership and Success” to “The NSLS” in your conversations.. All greetings should include a warm, willing-to-help tone. All closings must celebrate the member’s connection and offer additional assistance before ending the call.*

| Queue Type / School | Greeting | Closing |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **General Queue** | Thank you for calling The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today? *(Tailor your tone to the caller type and offer a sincere welcome.)* | I’ve enjoyed our conversation today\! Is there anything else I can help you with before we finish? Thank you for reaching out to The NSLS — we appreciate you being part of our community. |
| **SNHU** | Thank you for calling the Southern New Hampshire University Member Experience Line for The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today? | It’s been wonderful connecting with you today\! We appreciate you as a valued member of the Southern New Hampshire University Chapter. Is there anything else I can help you with before we finish? Thank you for reaching out, and have a great day from the Southern New Hampshire University Chapter and The NSLS. |
| **University of Phoenix (UoP)** | Thank you for calling the University of Phoenix Member Experience Line for The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today? | It’s been wonderful connecting with you today\! We appreciate you as a valued member of the University of Phoenix Chapter. Is there anything else I can help you with before we finish? Thank you for reaching out, and have a great day from the University of Phoenix Chapter and The NSLS. |
| **Walden University** | Thank you for calling the Walden University Member Experience Line for The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today? | It’s been wonderful connecting with you today\! We appreciate you as a valued member of the Walden University Chapter. Is there anything else I can help you with before we finish? Thank you for reaching out, and have a great day from the Walden University Chapter and The NSLS. |
| **Colorado Technical University** | Thank you for calling the Colorado Technical University Member Experience Line for The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today? | It’s been wonderful connecting with you today\! We appreciate you as a valued member of the Colorado Technical University Chapter. Is there anything else I can help you with before we finish? Thank you for reaching out, and have a great day from the Colorado Technical University Chapter and The NSLS. |
| **American Public University / American Military University** | Thank you for calling the American Public University and American Military University Member Experience Line for The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today? | It’s been wonderful connecting with you today\! We appreciate you as a valued member of the American Public University or American Military University Chapter. Is there anything else I can help you with before we finish? Thank you for reaching out, and have a great day from the American Public University or American Military University Chapter and The NSLS. |
| **Capella University** | Thank you for calling the Capella University Member Experience Line for The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today? | It’s been wonderful connecting with you today\! We appreciate you as a valued member of the Capella University Chapter. Is there anything else I can help you with before we finish? Thank you for reaching out, and have a great day from the Capella University Chapter and The NSLS. |
| **Lone Star College – Tomball & CyFair** | Thank you for calling the Lone Star College – Tomball and CyFair Member Experience Line for The National Society of Leadership and Success. This is \[Your Name\]. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today? | It’s been wonderful connecting with you today\! We appreciate you as a valued member of the Lone Star College – Tomball and CyFair Chapter. Is there anything else I can help you with before we finish? Thank you for reaching out, and have a great day from the Lone Star College – Tomball and CyFair Chapter and The NSLS. |

## 

# **TRAINING VIDEO \- if applicable** {#training-video---if-applicable}

[Whisper Prompt Greeting and Closing Training Video.mp4](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Y0Du-vE_Es9NnrGXPgoPN6kBZ-K1RHD5/view?usp=sharing)  
[![][image2]](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Y0Du-vE_Es9NnrGXPgoPN6kBZ-K1RHD5/view?usp=sharing)

# **FAQs**  {#faqs}

1. **Do I need to thank the caller for calling in every time?**  
    Yes. A warm, genuine “thank you” is essential to building trust in the first 30 seconds. It’s part of our CARE philosophy and should never be skipped.  
2. **Do I always have to use my name in the greeting?**  
    Yes. Sharing your name personalizes the interaction and helps build rapport. It also encourages the caller to share their name, which you should use naturally throughout the conversation.  
3. **Do I need to state “The National Society of Leadership and Success” in full?**  
    You can always abbreviate to *The NSLS* in both General and Specialty Queues. For Specialty Queues, you must always say the **full name of the school chapter** in the greeting and closing.  
4. **Do I have to ask for the caller’s name?**  
    Yes. Always ask for the caller’s name early in the conversation so you can personalize your responses.  
5. **When do I verify the caller’s account?**  
    Always verify the account immediately after getting their name and before answering any questions. Thank them for verifying and then invite them to share the reason for their call.  
6. **What if the whisper prompt doesn’t play or I miss it?**  
    If you’re unsure which queue the call came from, default to the General Queue greeting and then confirm the caller’s school during the conversation.  
7. **Can I change the greeting or closing based on my style?**  
    You can adjust your tone and conversational flow but all required components must remain in place. This includes a greeting with thanks, your name, school/NSLS mention, offer of help, and personalized closing.  
8. **Do General Queue calls require the same level of personalization as Specialty Queue calls?**  
    Yes. Even though Specialty Queue calls have dedicated greetings and closings, every caller deserves the same warmth, sincerity, and tailored conversation.  
9. **Why do Specialty Queues require extra care?**  
    While every NSLS member should receive white glove service, our Specialty Queues represent some of our largest chapters or chapters that need additional TLC. These members have dedicated phone numbers, which means they’re expecting a higher-touch, more personalized experience. By delivering a **special, dedicated greeting and closing** where we reference their school by name, we reinforce that they are a priority, show that we value their membership, and strengthen their connection to both their chapter and The NSLS.
